title: Ensure 'HTTPS Only' is set to 'On'

input_row.title: Ensure 'HTTPS Only' is set to 'On'
input_row.description: Azure App Service allows apps to run under both HTTP and HTTPS by default. Apps can be accessed by anyone using non-secure HTTP links by default. Non-secure HTTP requests can be restricted and all HTTP requests redirected to the secure HTTPS port. It is recommended to enforce HTTPS-only traffic.
input_row.rationale: Enabling HTTPS-only traffic will redirect all non-secure HTTP requests to HTTPS ports. HTTPS uses the TLS/SSL protocol to provide a secure connection which is both encrypted and authenticated. It is therefore important to support HTTPS for the security benefits.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Login to Azure Portal using https://portal.azure.com 2. Go to App Services 3. For each App Service 4. Under Setting section, click on Configuration 5. Under the General Settings tab, ensure that HTTPS Only is set to On under Platform Settings Audit from Azure CLI To check HTTPS-only traffic value for an existing app, run the following command, az webapp show --resource-group --name -- query httpsOnly The output should return true if HTTPS-only traffic value is set to On. Audit from PowerShell List all the web apps configured within the subscription. Get-AzWebApp | Select-Object ResourceGroup, Name, HttpsOnly For each web app review the HttpsOnly setting and make sure it is set to True. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: a4af4a39-4135-47fb-b175-47fbdf85311d - Name: 'App Service apps should only be accessible over HTTPS'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Login to Azure Portal using https://portal.azure.com 2. Go to App Services 3. For each App Service 4. Under Setting section, click on Configuration 5. Under the General Settings tab, set HTTPS Only to On under Platform Settings Remediate from Azure CLI To set HTTPS-only traffic value for an existing app, run the following command: az webapp update --resource-group --name -- set httpsOnly=true Remediate from PowerShell Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName -Name - HttpsOnly $true
input_row.impact: When it is enabled, every incoming HTTP request is redirected to the HTTPS port. This means an extra level of security will be added to the HTTP requests made to the app.
input_row.default_value: HTTPS Only is disabled by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_apigateway_api_https_only_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls/boto3 SDK methods:** Use the `get_rest_api` method to check the settings of API Gateway resources.
- **AWS CLI commands:** The command `aws apigateway get-rest-api --rest-api-id <api-id> --query policy` can be used to retrieve the API policy and check for HTTPS-only access.
- **Specific resources to enumerate:** All API Gateway rest APIs within a specified account and region.
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:** Look for the `policy` attribute to determine if HTTPS-only settings are enforced.
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:** The API should return a policy that includes a directive enforcing HTTPS only or contains no section permitting non-HTTPS traffic. If no HTTP access is allowed in the policy, it should be deemed compliant.
